_**Agent (claude-4.5-sonnet)**_

In this repo, your API key is deployed as a **Vercel server environment variable**, not in code.

- Set `OPENAI_API_KEY` in **Vercel → Project → Settings → Environment Variables** (Production/Preview/Development as needed).
- It’s read at runtime on the server via `process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY` in API routes (for example, `frontend/src/app/api/theory-inspector/route.ts`).
- Because it’s **not** prefixed with `NEXT_PUBLIC_`, it is **not exposed to the browser bundle**.
- Your docs already enforce this pattern in `docs/deployment.md` (`OPENAI_API_KEY` marked as secret; warning to never use `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` for keys).

Quick verification after deploy:
- Hit `GET /api/theory-inspector` on your deployed app; it reports `hasApiKey: true` when the key is present.

---

_**User**_

The deployment was blocked because the commit email thejeffginger@gmail.com could not be matched to a Git account. Ensure your git email matches your Git account.


---

_**Agent (claude-4.5-sonnet)**_

This is a **Vercel Git integration blocker**, not an API key issue. Vercel can't match your commit email to a GitHub account.

Two paths to fix:

## Option 1: Match your Git email to GitHub (recommended)

Check what GitHub expects:

```bash
# See what email you're using locally
git config user.email

# See what emails GitHub knows about
# Go to: https://github.com/settings/emails
```

Then either:

**A) Change your local Git email to match GitHub:**

```bash
cd /Users/shivpat/harmonyforge
git config user.email "your-github-email@example.com"
```

**B) Or add `thejeffginger@gmail.com` to your GitHub account:**

1. Visit https://github.com/settings/emails
2. Add `thejeffginger@gmail.com`
3. Verify it via the confirmation email GitHub sends

After matching, make a new commit (even a trivial one) and push:

```bash
git commit --allow-empty -m "Fix Git email for Vercel"
git push
```

---

## Option 2: Bypass (quick but not recommended)

If Vercel is blocking **only** on email mismatch:

1. Go to **Vercel → Project → Settings → Git**
2. Check if there's an "Allow unverified commits" or similar toggle
3. Or disconnect/reconnect Git integration to reset the link

---

**Why this happens:** Vercel tries to attribute deploys to a GitHub user. When the commit email doesn't match any GitHub account emails, it blocks the deploy for security/audit reasons.

_**Agent (claude-4.5-sonnet)**_

Done. This repo will now use `thejeffginger@gmail.com` for commits.

**Next steps to unblock Vercel:**

1. **Add that email to your GitHub account** (required for Vercel to accept it):
   - Visit https://github.com/settings/emails
   - Add `thejeffginger@gmail.com`
   - Verify it via the confirmation email

2. **Make a new commit** so Vercel sees the correct email:
   ```bash
   git commit --allow-empty -m "Configure Git email for Vercel"
   git push
   ```

Vercel should accept the new commit once GitHub knows about that email address.

_**Agent (claude-4.5-sonnet)**_

The commits are small fixes:
1. `d912f1a` - Fixed Makefile (16 lines changed)
2. `0f2f055` - Fixed lint error in RiffScoreEditor.tsx (8 lines changed)

**Two options:**

**Option A: Revert the commits** (safe, keeps history):
```bash
git revert --no-commit 0f2f055 d912f1a
git commit -m "Revert Jeff's commits to fix Vercel deploy"
git push
```

**Option B: Reset to before Jeff's commits** (cleaner, but rewrites history):
```bash
git reset --hard be6c858
git push --force
```

Which approach do you prefer? 

- **Option A** is safer if others have pulled these commits
- **Option B** is cleaner but requires force-push (risky if this is shared)

Or I can just change the **author** of those commits to your email without changing the code - that would fix the Vercel block while keeping the fixes.

---

_**User**_

Just change author to me

---

  ▲ vercel inspect url|deploymentId [options]

  Show information about a deployment.

  -F,  --format &lt;FORMAT&gt;  Specify the output format (json)                      
  -l,  --logs             Prints the build logs instead of the deployment

       --timeout &lt;TIME&gt;   Time to wait for deployment completion [3m]           
       --wait             Blocks until deployment completes                     


  Global Options:

       --cwd &lt;DIR&gt;            Sets the current working directory for a single

  -d,  --debug                Debug mode (default off)                          
  -Q,  --global-config &lt;DIR&gt;  Path to the global &#96;.vercel&#96; directory            
  -h,  --help                 Output usage information                          
  -A,  --local-config &lt;FILE&gt;  Path to the local &#96;vercel.json&#96; file

  -t,  --token &lt;TOKEN&gt;        Login token                                       
  -v,  --version              Output the version number                         


  Examples:

  - Get information about a deployment by its unique URL

    $ vercel inspect my-deployment-ji2fjij2.vercel.app

  - Get information about the deployment an alias points to

    $ vercel inspect my-deployment.vercel.app

  - Get information about a deployment by piping in the URL

    $ echo my-deployment.vercel.app | vercel inspect

  - Wait up to 90 seconds for deployment to complete

    $ vercel inspect my-deployment.vercel.app --wait --timeout 90s

  - Get deployment build logs

    $ vercel inspect my-deployment.vercel.app --logs

  - Get deployment information as JSON
